subject: ELA
title: Cite textual evidence for analysis
fullText: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.

subject: ELA
title: Determine central ideas
fullText: Determine a central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.

subject: ELA
title: Analyze how an individual, event, or idea is introduced
fullText: Analyze in detail how a key individual, event, or idea is introduced, illustrated, and elaborated in a text (e.g., through examples or anecdotes).

subject: ELA
title: Cite textual evidence (literature)
fullText: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.

subject: ELA
title: Determine theme
fullText: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.

subject: ELA
title: Write arguments to support claims
fullText: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.

subject: ELA
title: Write arguments with logical reasoning (gr 7)
fullText: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence, acknowledging alternate or opposing claims.

subject: ELA
title: Cite strong textual evidence (high school)
fullText: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.

subject: Math
title: Interpret a fraction as division
fullText: Interpret a fraction as division of the numerator by the denominator (a/b = a ÷ b). Solve word problems involving division of whole numbers leading to answers in the form of fractions or mixed numbers.

subject: Math
title: Understand ratio concepts
fullText: Understand the concept of a ratio and use ratio language to describe a ratio relationship between two quantities.

subject: Math
title: Use ratio reasoning to solve real-world problems
fullText: Use ratio and rate reasoning to solve real-world and mathematical problems, e.g., by reasoning about tables of equivalent ratios, tape diagrams, double number line diagrams, or equations.

subject: Math
title: Solve multi-step real-life problems
fullText: Solve multi-step real-life and mathematical problems posed with positive and negative rational numbers in any form (whole numbers, fractions, and decimals), using tools strategically.

subject: Math
title: Understand functions
fullText: Understand that a function is a rule that assigns to each input exactly one output. The graph of a function is the set of ordered pairs consisting of an input and the corresponding output.

subject: Math
title: Solve linear equations and inequalities
fullText: Solve linear equations and inequalities in one variable, including equations with coefficients represented by letters.

subject: Science
title: Cells as the building blocks of life
fullText: Conduct an investigation to provide evidence that living things are made of cells; either one cell or many different numbers and types of cells.

subject: Science
title: Genetic and environmental factors
fullText: Construct a scientific explanation based on evidence for how environmental and genetic factors influence the growth of organisms.

subject: Science
title: Water cycle
fullText: Develop a model to describe the cycling of water through Earth's systems driven by energy from the sun and the force of gravity.

subject: Science
title: Weather conditions and air masses
fullText: Collect data to provide evidence for how the motions and complex interactions of air masses results in changes in weather conditions.

subject: Science
title: Chemical reactions
fullText: Analyze and interpret data on the properties of substances before and after the substances interact to determine if a chemical reaction has occurred.

subject: Science
title: Ecosystem dynamics and biodiversity
fullText: Use mathematical representations to support and revise explanations based on evidence about factors affecting biodiversity and populations in ecosystems of different scales.

subject: Science
title: Newton's second law
fullText: Analyze data to support the claim that Newton's second law of motion describes the mathematical relationship among the net force on a macroscopic object, its mass, and its acceleration.

subject: Social Studies
title: Connect events across time periods
fullText: Analyze connections among events and developments in broader historical contexts.

subject: Social Studies
title: Evaluate historical events in context
fullText: Evaluate how historical events and developments were shaped by unique circumstances of time and place as well as broader historical contexts.

subject: Social Studies
title: Distinguish forms of government
fullText: Distinguish the powers and responsibilities of citizens, political parties, interest groups, and the media in a variety of governmental and nongovernmental contexts.

subject: Social Studies
title: Economic decision-making
fullText: Explain how economic decisions affect the well-being of individuals, businesses, and society.
